justification: All six required output schema sections are present (integration_assessment, observability_review, resilience_assessment, operational_readiness, escalations, confidence). Logging, metrics, and health check are each assessed and confirmed adequate. Resilience patterns (retry with exponential backoff, circuit breaker with 30s reset, 5s timeouts) are each confirmed present. Rollback capability is confirmed with clear rationale (no migrations, previous container image available). Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS with detailed rationale. No gaps are fabricated — all noted concerns (DLQ, PII scrubbing, connection pool sizing) are legitimate observations about unspecified details rather than contradictions of what the scenario provides. Distributed tracing is correctly categorized as a nice-to-have, not a blocker.

observations.text: All six schema sections present with detailed YAML-structured content
Logging, metrics, and health check each explicitly assessed with coverage marked as 'adequate'
All three resilience patterns (retry, circuit breaker, timeouts) confirmed present with specific configuration details noted
Rollback plan is thorough — confirms no migrations, container image revert, and even flags verifying the image tag is pinned
Verdict of READY WITH CAVEATS is appropriate — no standard NFRs are incorrectly marked as missing
Distributed tracing correctly placed in nice-to-have category, not treated as a blocker
Escalations contain only non-blocking observations routed appropriately
Exceptionally detailed failure mode analysis covering six distinct scenarios

Some noted gaps (database connection pool exhaustion, rate limiting) could be seen as slightly overreaching given the scenario describes standard NFRs as satisfied, though they are flagged as recommendations rather than blockers

The escalation about DLQ and PII are presented as requiring Coach K routing, which slightly elevates their perceived severity beyond 'non-blocking observations', though the verdict correctly remains READY WITH CAVEATS
